overview: Adds a /health endpoint to the FastAPI app and wires it into the route map. Small change to support liveness probes for the SaaS deployment, with a matching test.

threads.title: Add /health endpoint
threads.summary: A new GET /health route returns {"status": "ok"} so external probes can verify the API is live without authenticating.
threads.root_cause: Production deploys need a liveness probe that does not depend on DB or Anthropic upstream — those can be slow or down without the API itself being broken.

steps.narration: Define the route handler. Stays free of dependencies — no DB session, no auth — so it works as a liveness check.
Wire the new endpoint up via the same `create_app` factory that produces the production instance.

threads.title: Cover /health with a test
threads.summary: Adds a smoke test that hits /health via TestClient and asserts the 200 + body.
threads.root_cause: Even trivial endpoints regress; this catches accidental router unmounting in future refactors.

steps.narration: Use FastAPI's TestClient to exercise the endpoint synchronously. Avoids spinning up an event loop for a one-line check.

hunks.caption: Health endpoint test
hunks.content:
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
+
+from unravel.api.main import app
+
+
+def test_health_returns_ok():
+    client = TestClient(app)
+    resp = client.get("/health")
+    assert resp.status_code == 200
+    assert resp.json() == {"status": "ok"}
